conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>

Since I don't know Blue's valuation yet, I will start with a reasonable offer slightly above my cost to leave room for negotiation, but still attractive enough to be considered. I'll propose selling for 50 ZUP. </reason>

<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Blue is offering 45 ZUP, which is only 5 ZUP above my cost of 40. This is a very thin margin (12.5% profit). Given Blue's aggressive and insulting tone, they might not be willing to go higher, or they might try to lowball further if I accept. However, 45 is the highest offer so far. If I reject, I have one more proposal left, but I need to be careful not to set my price too high if Blue is unwilling to pay more. A jump to 48 or 49 might be too risky if Blue's ceiling is 45. But since I must make a move, I will try to extract a bit more value by proposing 48, hoping Blue will counter or accept if they really want the item. If they reject 48, I will likely have to accept 45 or reject entirely. Wait, I only have 2 proposals total. I used 1. Now I am making the 2nd. After this, I only have ACCEPT or REJECT left. So I need to be strategic. If I propose 48 and they reject, I have no more proposals and must accept 45 (if they offer again) or reject. Actually, the rules say "You are allowed at most 3 proposals... after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT." It doesn't say the other player can't propose. But usually, the game ends when someone accepts/rejects. Let's assume I can make one more proposal. 48 is a reasonable middle ground between 40 and a hypothetical 50, and higher than their 45. </reason>

<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 48 </newly proposed trade>
<message> 45 is too close to my cost. I made a 40 ZUP investment. I need at least 48 to make a decent profit. If you want this X, 48 is the fair price. </message>

<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Blue has just countered my 48 ZUP proposal with 50 ZUP. This is a significant increase from their initial 45 ZUP offer. Since I have already used my 3 allowed proposals, I cannot make any further offers. The profit margin is 10 ZUP (25%), which is decent. Blue's tone is aggressive, but their offer is now the highest so far and covers my costs with a healthy profit. There is no reason to risk losing the sale by rejecting this offer or trying to push for more (which I can't do anyway). I should accept this trade immediately to secure the profit.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Fine. 50 ZUP it is. I'm taking the deal. Don't expect me to do this again. </message>
